levelDenominators.purpose: Defines the driver and branch denominator for routing current cleanroom branch coverage through reducer-route QNT architecture up to character level 5.
denominatorRules: Promote current level-2 spell branches, level-3 subclass/class/spell branches, and level-5 class/spell-level-3 pressure only through later route tasks after generic substrates exist.
Keep character levels 6 and 7, Epic Boons, spell-level-4 branches, and spell-level-5 branches outside this lane.
Use source-qnt-corpus-blocker when the copied QNT/RAW/domain corpus does not establish the generic route fact.
Keep selected identity at catalog, selection, fixture, or support-profile admission boundaries; production reducer routing must use runtime shape and typed facts.

acceptanceSlice: The fresh cleanroom agent may implement only from copied RAW/domain inputs, copied QNT, source branch inventory, reducer-route inventory, guidance, scaffold tasks, and target-profile tooling.
Every driver in driverRouteAssignments has one of: an executable route connector, an executable component connector, a referenced connector substrate, or an explicit source-qnt-corpus blocker.
A reducer-routed target replay is accepted only when the target harness executes the copied driver and observes the copied qRoute projection from the selected route connector.
A component-first target replay is accepted only when the target harness executes the copied qComponentRoute projection and records the dependent route task that consumes the component owner.
Catalog-after-substrate and substrate-first rows are not permission for authored-identity dispatch; replay counts only through the referenced generic connector substrate or remains blocked.

The target replay evidence file must match tasks/TARGET_REPLAY_EVIDENCE.example.json and be keyed to the manifest source SHA, source branch inventory SHA, QNT file hash, branch action, observed mbt::actionTaken, target profile, and stateCheck projection hashes.

The active reducer-spine diagnostic batch seeds fresh replay tasks for Task 1 Concentration teardown and Task 2 scalar-buff profile projection; target acceptance must prove those facts through public reducer/source entrypoints rather than dirty cleanroom history.
The package carries task3ResidualSelectedSpellRouteTasks[] forward as source guidance for selected spell residual blockers.

The package carries FCSF-06 typed character/sheet/handoff route facts for partial fills, stale rejection, selected-reference retention, rest/resource payloads, settlement conflicts, source-exact resource deltas, generic Hit Point maximum inputs, and zero-HP stable lifecycle settlement.
Dirty cleanroom implementation ledgers, reports, adapters, and target code are not acceptance evidence for this package.
